published_prior_art.cite: MiaoGengTchetgen2018 and MiaoShiLiTchetgen2024: bridge identification under proxy/negative-control conditions; OUP https://academic.oup.com/biomet/article/105/4/987/5073056; DOI page https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/24754269.2024.2390748

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_did_bridge_frontier_v2 angle1: finite proximal-DiD regime atlas / diagnostic disagreement frontier

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2
novelty_flags.one_line: Exhibit 9.1 computes the bridge algebra, but the load-bearing tau_PT(P)=1/4 and theta_ATT=-1/20 are inserted as latent-completion values rather than exhibited by a full finite law from Section 6 primitives; Sigma(P) is therefore not fully computed from raw observed cells.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The handoff maps ZhangZhangWangEtAl2025 and RambachanRoth2023 to Theorem 2 as strict tightening/non-representability, but the Section 8 theorem is only a rational bridge-only witness and is not phrased as a strict result against either published comparator; Nature source https://www.nature.com/articles/s41746-025-01821-w and ReStud source https://academic.oup.com/restud/article/90/5/2555/7039335.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship non-nesting claim cites SoferRichardsonColicinoSchwartzTchetgen2016, RichardsonYeTchetgen2023, and TchetgenParkRichardson2024 without a section, equation, page, or quoted open-problem anchor tying those papers to the exact double-proxy bridge gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The generic open-regime claim has only a bridge-only Exhibit 9.1 witness; it does not exhibit concrete both, PT-only, and neither laws in the common finite support.

novelty_flags.label: proposal
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; Sigma(P) is a named signature, but the current contribution is a finite diagnostic packaging plus one witness, not a flagship-grade new identification target, estimator frontier, or published-comparator iff frontier.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Section 4 says Sigma(P) assigns every positive finite law to PT-only, bridge-only, both, or neither, but the Section 8 theorem excludes tau_PT from the observable computable components and the regime list omits rho_br=0 with kappa_br=0.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The four regimes are claimed to be open relative to a common positive-cell finite latent-type model, but the PT and bridge regimes are equality sets tau_PT=0 and rho_br=0; no model topology or component restriction is specified that makes those equality-defined regimes open.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The theorem asserts existence of a positive-cell observed law and finite latent completion on the full locked 2x3 binary-proxy support, but Exhibit 9.1 displays only selected margins and means, not a complete joint finite law or latent cell table.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Exhibit 9.1 with a full rational joint table for G, all period-specific W/Z/Y cells, U, and potential outcomes, then recompute tau_PT, theta_ATT, B_P, r_P, c_P, Delta_DiD, and Delta_br from that table.

Either remove the claim that Sigma(P) is computed from raw observed cells, or redefine the observable signature to exclude tau_PT and reserve PT classification for maintained assumptions or full-law witnesses.

For flagship targeting, restate the main conjecture as an iff or strict frontier against named published procedures, with section/equation anchors in MiaoShiLiTchetgen2024, ZhangZhangWangEtAl2025, and RambachanRoth2023.
Add concrete rational witnesses for the both, PT-only, and neither regimes, or downgrade Conjecture 1 from a generic open-regime atlas to the single bridge-only witness result.

recommended_next_step: Reject this angle as written; a viable replacement needs a fully exhibited finite law and a comparator-anchored flagship frontier rather than a finite diagnostic signature.
